query: Review this campaign idea: We want to send a printed postcard with a QR code to VP HRs at mid-market retail companies, offering a free audit of their employee turnover risk.
expected_behavior: Deconstructs the printed postcard idea into a clean core hypothesis format
Provides a strong, damaging counterargument focusing on direct-mail friction or QR-code security fears
Audits unproven assumptions such as VP HR physical address accuracy or QR code scanning permissions
Expands the concept into adjacent digital channels and a contrarian 'anti-postcard' strategy
Establishes a lean execution plan with an explicit week-by-week sequencing and a hard kill threshold
